title: key_cache does not validate server-returned KeyId is non-zero
description: key_cache() in key.rs initializes key_id = 0 (WH_KEYID_ERASED) and passes it to wh_Client_KeyCache, trusting the server to overwrite it with a valid non-zero id. If the server returns key_id=0 (e.g. a misbehaving or emulated server, firmware bug, or fuzzing scenario), KeyId(0) == KeyId::ERASED is stored. The Drop impl then never fires the leak warning. All subsequent crypto calls using id=0 will fail with opaque HSM errors rather than a clear 'invalid key' diagnosis. No assertion or check that key_id \!= 0 after the FFI call. Same pattern affects wolfhsm_rsa_make_key, wolfhsm_ecc_make_key, wolfhsm_ed25519_make_key, wolfhsm_curve25519_make_key, and wolfhsm_mldsa_make_key.
